steps.title: Locate the surprise
steps.content: The passage states that the map surprisingly revealed a magnetic field with no clear pattern or direction. That surprised researchers because they expected a more uniform field like those seen in other galactic bones.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported details
steps.content: Choice A invents inaccurate previous maps. Choice B says the measurements were similar to other galactic bones, the opposite of the passage. Choice D mentions field strength, but the surprise involved pattern and direction.

steps.title: Choose lack of uniformity
steps.content: A field with no clear pattern or direction is a field that is not uniform, which directly states what was surprising about the mapping. The answer is C.
